name: Basic Rock Beat
description: 8th notes on hi-hat, snare on 2 and 4, kick on 1 and 3

teacher_notes.beginner: Lock in the hi-hat first, then add snare on 2 and 4. Kick comes last. Play with a click.
teacher_notes.intermediate: The groove should feel like one unit, not three separate things. Relax your shoulders.
teacher_notes.advanced: Experiment with slight variations in hi-hat velocity. Not every 8th note needs to be identical.

name: Basic Funk Beat
description: 16th note hi-hat feel, displaced kick, ghost notes on snare

teacher_notes.beginner: Start without the ghost note. Get the kick pattern locked in with the hi-hat first.
teacher_notes.intermediate: Ghost notes should be barely audible — maybe 20% of the volume of the backbeat snare.
teacher_notes.advanced: The 'e' of beat 2 kick is what makes funk feel like it's pulling forward. Trust the pocket.

name: Jazz Ride Pattern
description: Classic jazz ride cymbal pattern: long-short-long-short

teacher_notes.beginner: Say 'spang-a-lang' out loud. That's the rhythm. Don't think about triplets — just feel it.
teacher_notes.intermediate: The ride should sound like it's floating, not hammering. Tip of the stick on the bow, or bell on uptempo tunes.
teacher_notes.advanced: The magic is in the swing. The 'and' of each beat should sit just slightly behind where you'd expect it.

name: Shuffle Groove
description: Triplet-based shuffle feel on hi-hat with standard backbeat

teacher_notes.beginner: This is a triplet feel — the hi-hat plays beats 1 and 3 of each triplet, skipping the middle. Like a horse trot.
teacher_notes.intermediate: The shuffle lives between straight and swung. Medium shuffle feels heavier and more earthbound than jazz swing.
teacher_notes.advanced: Lighten up the hi-hat strokes on the 'and' of each beat for a more sophisticated shuffle voice.

name: Half-Time Groove
description: Snare moves to beat 3 instead of 2 and 4 — creates a spacious, heavy feel

teacher_notes.beginner: Everything feels twice as slow. The snare on beat 3 should feel like an event, not a backbeat.
teacher_notes.intermediate: Half-time grooves are about what you don't play. Leave space. Let the kick do the talking.
teacher_notes.advanced: Add ghost notes on the snare before beat 3 to create forward motion in the phrase.
